codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of the weekly menu page. The page at /menu correctly displays Monday–Friday meals organized by day with section headers, meal cards in a responsive grid, a VIEW NEXT WEEK placeholder, and a CTA section linking to /meal-plans/order. The code follows project conventions (Tailwind utility classes, Next.js Image, Button component with as="a", label-text class, section-padding, max-container, data-placeholder attribute, font-heading/font-body usage, and zero border-radius). Data is sourced from existing WEEKLY_MENU constants. TypeScript and lint pass cleanly. Only minor non-blocking observations.

issues.description: The "use client" directive is used solely because the VIEW NEXT WEEK button has an onClick handler (alert). Since this is a placeholder, the component could be a server component if the placeholder used a <Link> or plain anchor instead. This would improve performance by reducing the client bundle size. Minor optimization opportunity.

The VIEW NEXT WEEK placeholder uses alert() which is a jarring UX. The feature description says 'non-functional, placeholder' which could be implemented as a disabled link or a button with no action rather than an alert. However, this matches the project's TODO pattern for backend integration placeholders, so it's acceptable.

Image aspect ratio is 4:3 (landscape) whereas AGENTS.md says 'Product images: 3:4 aspect ratio on light gray (#F7F7F7) background'. However, these are food/meal photos (not product cards), and 4:3 landscape is appropriate for food photography. Other non-product images in the codebase also use 4:3 (CategoryNav, EditorialCards). The bg-gray-light (#F7F7F7) background is correctly applied. This is a reasonable deviation.

The VIEW NEXT WEEK section uses custom padding classes (px-6 md:px-16) instead of section-padding. This creates a slight visual inconsistency with other sections' horizontal padding, though since it's a single-line centered link this is a minor cosmetic concern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states 'Product images: 3:4 aspect ratio on light gray (#F7F7F7) background' but this rule is specific to product cards. Meal/food images throughout the site use 4:3 (landscape) aspect ratios (CategoryNav, EditorialCards, and now WeeklyMenuContent). AGENTS.md could clarify that the 3:4 ratio applies specifically to product catalog cards, while food/editorial images use 4:3.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/menu/WeeklyMenuContent.tsx:45 uses aspect-[4/3], src/components/sections/CategoryNav.tsx:28 uses aspect-[4/3], src/components/sections/EditorialCards.tsx:34 uses aspect-[4/3], while src/components/sections/ProductCarousel.tsx:95 uses aspect-[3/4].

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used 'use client' for the entire WeeklyMenuContent component solely to support an alert() call in a placeholder button. AGENTS.md says 'Use "use client" directive only when needed (interactive components)' but doesn't provide guidance on whether placeholder interactivity justifies client-side rendering of an otherwise static page. A pattern note about keeping large static sections as server components and isolating small interactive bits into their own client components could help future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/menu/WeeklyMenuContent.tsx:1 — 'use client' directive. The only interactivity is the onClick handler at line 119 that calls alert().

summary: The weekly menu page implementation is solid and passes review. It correctly displays all 5 weekdays of meals from the WEEKLY_MENU constants, with proper day headers, responsive meal card grids (1-col mobile, 2-col tablet, 4-col desktop), Unsplash food images via Next.js Image, meal type labels, the VIEW NEXT WEEK placeholder, and a CTA section with 'Ready to Eat This Good?' heading linking to /meal-plans/order. The code follows project conventions for Tailwind styling, component structure (feature-specific directory), Button usage, and SEO metadata. TypeScript and lint checks pass. The only snack item appears on Monday (matching the Full Alignment plan distinction). No blocking issues found — only minor non-blocking observations about 'use client' scope and placeholder UX.
